title: Schatz der Welten: Untergang
abstract: In a world of mystery and magic, a young adventurer takes on the quest, to search for a long-forgotten treasure in the wilderness of the Durabor. Soon he experiences the magical wonders of the fantastic realm – and the demonic dangers lingering beneath. Just as he sets foot into the sprawling forest, dark forces rise from the underworld, threatening to claim the lands of the flourishing kingdom of Norskom. – A danger, that only the keen adventurers in the wilderness can confine, as they strive to protect their mystical world. Untergang is a gripping opening to Joachim Schiller's epic fantasy trilogy, rich with myth, betrayal, and the long shadow of fallen empires.

title: Schatz der Welten: Norskoms Rache
abstract: The second book in the Schatz der Welten trilogy. Hoping to have expelled the evil from the Durabor, the kingdom of Norskom longes for new safety. But the sinister desires of Mardok, the god of war, force the worlds into new chaos. Soon, new challenges emerge from the underworld, that convey a clear message: the darkness has not been defeated yet! Norskoms Rache raises the stakes of the trilogy to breathtaking heights, deepening the world's mythology while delivering relentless momentum.

title: Schatz der Welten: Die gefallenen Elben
abstract: The stunning conclusion to Joachim Schiller's Schatz der Welten trilogy. The truth about the fallen elves – a civilisation driven by dark magic – is finally revealed. Alongside the true power of the gods, that decide over the mystical Durabors fate. The adventurers of the human kingdom of Norskom are ground down between their inner struggles, and the devastating plans of Mardok, the god of war, as they hope to guide their world into a new peaceful future. Die gefallenen Elben is an epic, emotionally devastating finale that rewards every page of the journey.

title: Geheimcode WorldSkills
abstract: When a stolen prototype disappears from the WorldSkills competition venue on the eve of the opening ceremony, detective Steven Plodowski finds himself thrust into a world of gifted young craftspeople, corporate espionage, and international intrigue. The thief is somewhere among thousands of competitors, experts, and officials from across the globe – and Plodowski has seventy-two hours before the prototype is used for something far more dangerous than winning a medal. A fast-paced thriller that takes readers inside the breathtaking world of the world vocational championships.

title: Die Rache der Schwabenkinder
abstract: Steven Plodowski ermittelt 2. A history teacher is found dead in a rural Swabian schoolhouse, clutching a seventeenth-century document about the infamous Schwabenkinder – children sent from Vorarlberg and Tyrol to work as farmhands in Swabia each spring. When a second death follows, Plodowski begins to suspect that centuries-old grievances have not died with the history books. The second Plodowski novel is darker and more personal than the first, weaving history into an unsettling present-day mystery.

title: Konstanz – Tod am See: Das Vermächtnis des Jan Hus
abstract: Steven Plodowski ermittelt 4. A theologian is pulled from the Bodensee carrying a medieval letter that was never supposed to surface – a letter that implicates the Council of Constance in a cover-up surrounding the execution of Jan Hus in 1415. Six hundred years later, someone is prepared to kill again to keep the secret buried. Plodowski's most historically ambitious case takes him to the stunning shores of Lake Constance, where the past has never been more dangerous.

title: Schwäbisches Gold
abstract: Steven Plodowski ermittelt 3. A vintage car restoration workshop in the Swabian Alb is destroyed by fire – but not before someone removes what it was secretly storing for decades. The trail leads Plodowski through the post-war black market, a network of ageing car enthusiasts, and a hidden fortune whose owners never expected to be found. A sun-drenched Swabian summer noir.

title: Ein letztes Bild
abstract: A retired art conservator is found dead in her Stuttgart apartment, surrounded by photographs of a painting that officially doesn't exist. Plodowski's investigation unearths a wartime theft, a forged provenance, and a family that has protected a lie for eighty years. Ein letztes Bild is Paul Steinbeck's most intimate novel – a meditation on art, memory, and the weight of inherited guilt.

title: Marcs Suche
abstract: Fourteen-year-old Marc has one summer to find out what really happened to his grandfather – a man erased from every family photograph. A coming-of-age mystery that takes Marc through village archives, overgrown cemeteries, and the reluctant memories of very old people. Paul Steinbeck's warmest and most hopeful book.

title: Der Flug des Zitronenfalters: Die große Wende 2098
abstract: 2098. Germany's last functioning power grid has just gone dark. In a city held together by the expertise of its last generation of skilled tradespeople, one engineer refuses to accept that civilisation is over. A rare venture into speculative fiction from Steinbeck – and a love letter to the dignity of hands-on work.

title: Határon Át / Across the Border
abstract: Two siblings separated by the Iron Curtain in 1956 find each other again sixty years later – but the woman who answers the door is not who either of them expected. A quiet, devastating novel about identity, language, and the borders we carry inside ourselves. Published simultaneously in Hungarian, German, English, and Dutch.

title: A Tanú / Der Zeuge / The Witness
abstract: A celebrated architect is asked to testify at a war crimes tribunal about a building she designed – a building that was later used for purposes she claims she never intended. Told in four languages across four timelines, A Tanú is a formally daring novel about complicity, beauty, and whether a structure can be guilty of the acts committed inside it.

title: De Codekloof / The Code Divide
abstract: When a whistleblower leaks evidence that an AI training platform used at vocational colleges across Europe has been systematically discriminating against applicants from working-class backgrounds, a young data journalist and a disgraced software engineer race to expose the truth before the system's creators bury it permanently. A razor-sharp tech thriller that asks who decides which skills the future is allowed to value.

title: De Laatste Frequentie / The Last Frequency
abstract: A radio telescope in the Dutch polders picks up a signal that cannot be natural – and cannot, according to every expert consulted, be artificial either. The physicist tasked with analysing it begins to suspect the signal is not from space but from the deep past. A mesmerising blend of hard science and philosophical mystery, The Last Frequency asks what we would do if we received a message from a civilisation that no longer existed.

title: The Tide Warden
abstract: On the remote island of Haafey, a young woman inherits her grandmother's role as keeper of the tidal records – ledgers that have been kept continuously for two hundred years. When the most recent entries reveal something impossible, she must choose between the safety of the known and the truth waiting at the edge of the sea. Longlisted for the Booker Prize, The Tide Warden is a novel of quiet devastation.

title: The Ferryman's Daughter
abstract: In a small Scottish coastal town in 1972, the ferryman's daughter witnesses something she can't explain – and spends the next fifty years trying to. Told in alternating timelines, The Ferryman's Daughter is a haunting investigation into how a single moment can reshape an entire life, and how long a community can protect a shared silence.

title: Das Schweigen der Akten
abstract: A Leipzig archivist discovers that a file relating to his own family has been removed from the Stasi records – removed not after reunification, but six years before the Wall fell. His attempt to reconstruct what the file contained leads him into a web of informants, betrayals, and a love story that the state tried very hard to destroy. A debut of extraordinary restraint and power.

title: Die letzte Zeugin
abstract: She is ninety-one years old and has never spoken about what she saw in Leipzig in October 1989. Now a documentary filmmaker wants her story, and she must decide what truth is owed to history – and what belongs to the dead. Wengler's most emotionally devastating novel is also his shortest, and his best.

title: Skills of Gold
abstract: Five competitors. Five countries. One competition that will change all of them forever. Skills of Gold follows a young electrician from London, a carpenter from rural Japan, a plumber from Lagos, a pastry chef from Lyon, and a mechatronics specialist from São Paulo as they converge on a WorldSkills competition – and discover that the skills being tested are not always the ones that matter most. A joyful, urgent novel about excellence, belonging, and what it means to be the best at something the world has forgotten to value.

title: The Voltage Between Us
abstract: Zara is back – now a WorldSkills expert judge, watching the next generation compete with a mixture of pride and unease. When a competitor from a country on the brink of civil war goes missing the night before the finals, Zara must decide how far outside her remit she is willing to go. A thrilling and deeply humane sequel to Skills of Gold.
